figures.caption: Table 11: AMIYA data used for post-training and how it was augmented to make instruction-following data.

Figure 2: IDIOLEX training framework. During training, all batches are sampled such that every individual item can act as an anchor for contrastive learning, necessitating that there are 23−n samples for each proximity score n ∈ [0, 3].
Table 4: Data split sizes for Reddit data by dialect.
Table 7: Training hyperparameters used for IDIOLEX.

Table 3: Dialectalness (ADI2) and translation performance (ChrF++) scores on the AMIYA shared task evaluation set for Moroccan (mar), Egyptian (egy), Palestinian (pse), Syrian (syr), and Saudi (sau) Arabic. Our models, particularly Allam + IDIOLEX SFT, balance style and translation quality across dialects, as compared to reported scores for top submissions for the AMIYA shared task (Robinson et al., 2026) (bottom three rows). Best scores from our experiments are bolded, while best reported scores from the AMIYA shared task are underlined.
Table 5: Spanish linguistic features extracted via LLM for IDIOLEX training. All features are sentencelevel binary indicators.
Table 10: Clustering and retrieval-based evaluation of dialectal structure in sentence embedding spaces.
Table 6: Arabic linguistic features extracted via LLM for IDIOLEX training. All features are sentencelevel binary indicators.
